commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=c3db53d5c920c1a6e993447236da18816e1dfdd3)
commands.review_summary:
The PR moves `scripts/install.sh` Homebrew setup from unconditional preparation into the missing/outdated Node branch and updates one installer ordering test regex.

PR surface: Tests 0, Other 0. Total 0 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible: current `main()` calls `install_homebrew` before `check_node`, so a no-Homebrew non-admin macOS user exits before the adequate Node check. I did not run a live non-admin macOS installer session in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: Contributor or maintainer follow-up is needed because the patch has a concrete macOS installer blocker and the contributor still needs to supply real installer proof.

target.title: fix(install): skip Homebrew when adequate Node is already present
target.body:
## Summary

`install.sh` calls `install_homebrew` unconditionally before checking whether Node.js is already available. On macOS without admin rights and without Homebrew, this aborts the entire installation even when the existing Node satisfies the minimum version requirement (22.19+).

The user sees:

```
[1/3] Preparing environment
· Homebrew not found, installing
· Installing Homebrew
✗ Installing Homebrew failed
Need sudo access on macOS (e.g. the user needs to be an Administrator)!
```

The workaround (`npm i -g openclaw@latest`) works, confirming brew is not needed when Node is present.

## Fix

Move `check_node` before `install_homebrew` so the Homebrew step is only reached when Node actually needs to be installed. Users with an adequate Node on PATH skip straight to the npm install stage.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: install.sh runs install_homebrew even when Node >= 22.19 is on PATH, blocking non-admin macOS users
Real environment tested: macOS arm64, Node 26, built from patched source checkout
Exact steps or command run after this patch: node -e script parsing install.sh main() to verify call ordering and guard structure
Evidence after fix:
```
BEFORE (origin/main) call order:
  1. install_homebrew
  2. load_nvm_for_node_detection
  3. check_node
  4. install_node
install_homebrew: UNCONDITIONAL (runs even when Node is adequate)

AFTER (this patch) call order:
  1. load_nvm_for_node_detection
  2. check_node
  3. install_homebrew
  4. install_node
install_homebrew is inside the "if ! check_node" branch.
Users with adequate Node skip Homebrew entirely.
```
Observed result after fix: install_homebrew moved inside the check_node guard; users with adequate Node on PATH skip straight to npm install without requiring Homebrew or admin rights
What was not tested: Live non-admin macOS install (requires account without sudo)

Closes #83232
target.author: sebtardif
